diagrams.title: Database Access Control Models Comparison
diagrams.description: A comparison table showing DAC, MAC, RBAC, and ABAC models with their characteristics
elements: MAC: System-controlled, strict classification, high security
RBAC: Role-based, scalable, supports least privilege
ABAC: Attribute-based, highly granular, dynamic policies
diagrams.purpose: Understand differences between access control models and when to use each

diagrams.title: RBAC Implementation Flow
diagrams.description: Flow diagram showing how users get access through roles in RBAC
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how Role-Based Access Control works in practice

diagrams.title: SQL Injection Attack Process
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how SQL injection attack occurs through user input
diagrams.purpose: Understand SQL injection attack mechanism and prevention methods

diagrams.title: Database Security Layers
diagrams.description: Multi-layer security approach for database protection
elements: Layer 5: Physical Security and Network Security
diagrams.purpose: Visualize defense-in-depth approach to database security
